metadata.title: WCN — The Business Network for Web3 and AI
metadata.description: WCN connects institutional capital, projects, and services into one network — with Proof of Business verification and fair settlement for every contributor.
metadata.ogDescription: The business network for Web3 and AI: real operators, verified outcomes, fair settlement.
metadata.twitterDescription: Real operators. Verified outcomes. Fair settlement. The business network for Web3 and AI.

footer.tagline: The business network for Web3 and AI. Real operators, verified outcomes, fair settlement.
footer.motto: The whole institution is one proof ledger.
footer.copyright: © {year} Web3 Capital Network. All rights reserved.
footer.colophon: Set in EB Garamond & JetBrains Mono · Designed per the WCN Visual System · Anno MMXXVI
footer.legal: This site is not an offer of securities, investment advice, or legal counsel. Node rights and any future token are characterized by external counsel in each applicable jurisdiction. Not open to US Persons during Phase 1–2.

home.eyebrow: The Business Network for Web3 and AI
home.headline: Capital meets proof.
home.lede: WCN is the global coordination protocol connecting nodes, agents, and capital for the AI era. Resources flow around verifiable business outcomes — auditable from registry to settlement.
home.innovationsTitle: Three foundations on a single logic chain.
home.innovationsDesc: The node network, AI agents, and Proof of Business sit on one logic chain — the fundamental difference between WCN and any single-point solution.
home.innovation1Body: 100 Founding + Country + City + Track + Service + Standard form a six-tier pyramid. The top four tiers are partner contracts that bind rights to responsibility.
home.innovation2Body: Agents are structured execution units, not tools. Each carries a capability manifest, a task contract, and PoB receipts.
home.innovation3Body: A proof mechanism for real business outcomes. Not points, not activity — outcome evidence. No PoB, no settlement.
home.innovationsCaption: Node → Agent → PoB · one logic chain
home.ledgersInMotionDesc: Work enters the project ledger. Capital matches it in the capital ledger. Outcomes are verified and sealed in the proof ledger. Every step is auditable, every contributor recognized.
home.ledgersInMotionCaption: A live cycle: project → capital → proof.
home.manifestoBody: should be earned by evidence, not granted by reputation. The global economy is moving onchain. Coordination should follow.
home.loopDesc: Every opportunity becomes structured work. Every outcome is verified. Every contributor is recognized.
home.stepTaskDesc: Work is assigned, scoped, and executed
home.ctaBandTitle: Become a node in the network.
home.ctaBandDesc: Apply to operate as a verified node, propose a deal, or run service workflows backed by Proof of Business.

about.title: About
about.metaDesc: WCN is the business network for Web3 and AI: real operators organize resources, AI agents scale execution, Proof of Business verifies outcomes, and settlement rewards contribution.
about.headline: One network. Real participants. Verified outcomes.
about.lede: Web3 Capital Network is a business network for the Web3 and AI economy — where real operators connect capital to execution, every outcome is backed by evidence, and settlement traces back to who actually delivered.
about.subLede: WCN does not start from a chain and hope for adoption. It starts from organizing real participants, real tasks, and real evidence — then lets identity, assets, and settlement build on verified history.
about.pillarNodeBody: Operators who bring capital, projects, services, and regional reach — each accountable for what they route into the network.
about.pillarAgentBody: Research, outreach, and operations that run inside task workflows with full permissions and audit trails.
about.pillarPobBody: Only verified, evidence-backed outcomes earn a place in the record. PoB is the gate between operations and long-term value.
about.definitionTitle: What WCN is — and what it's not just
about.definitionDesc: The clearest way to understand WCN is to see what it does, and what it deliberately avoids.
about.whatWcnIsLede: A network that connects capital, resources, and execution — with evidence-backed proof and fair settlement.
about.whatWcnIsNotLede: WCN touches all of these, but if you define it by any single one, you'll miss the full picture.
about.whyNowDesc: Several structural changes in the market are landing together. WCN is built to respond to all of them.
about.diffDesc: Most platforms handle one piece — fundraising, or execution, or reporting. WCN connects all four into a single chain of accountability.
about.measureDesc: WCN tracks who moved the outcome — not who was loudest in the room.
about.foundationDesc: Four operating principles that guide every product decision.
about.ctaDesc: Explore the architecture, the node system, and the Proof of Business model — or apply to join the network.
about.whatIsBullet1: Connects projects, capital, services, and execution in one workflow — not a passive directory.
about.whatIsBullet2: Starts from real business activity and accountable operators. On-chain identity follows when the network earns it.
about.whatIsBullet3: Combines human judgment with AI execution. Evidence and review come before anything becomes a record.
about.whatIsNotBullet1: Not a blockchain launched to find use cases later.
about.whatIsNotBullet2: Not a points-for-posting community. Activity alone does not create value.
about.whatIsNotBullet3: Not a badge you can buy. Nodes are reviewed, accountable seats.
about.whatIsNotBullet4: Not a yield or guaranteed-return token product.
about.whatIsNotBullet5: Not a single-purpose fund or placement service.
about.whatIsNotBullet6: Not buzzword stacking. Every layer answers: who did the work, what was proven, and how credit is assigned.
about.window1Body: The market has shifted. Winning is no longer about launching a new chain — it is about organizing capital, markets, and execution end to end.
about.window2Body: AI agents can execute at scale, but without attributable tasks and verified evidence, their output cannot be trusted or settled.
about.window3Body: Investors and partners increasingly ask what actually shipped — not impressions, narratives, or vanity metrics.
about.window4Body: RWA, stablecoins, and institutional expectations make on-chain settlement credible — once proof of work exists.
about.window5Body: The tooling is mature enough to build nodes, tasks, evidence, and proof before forcing token-led distribution.
about.struct1Body: Real operators who introduce capital, projects, and services — and take responsibility for what happens next.
about.struct2Body: AI automation inside task workflows and audit trails, amplifying humans while keeping accountability intact.
about.struct3Body: Verified results become durable records — the input for reputation, trust, and future settlement.
about.struct4Body: Allocation, staking, and governance are all tied to verified contribution — never decoupled from evidence.
about.principle1Body: Align participants around executable work — not a list of logos that never ships together.
about.principle2Body: Claims need evidence, review, and attribution. Without that, everything collapses into noise.
about.principle3Body: Long-term value should track verified contribution, not whoever talks loudest.
about.principle4Body: Every node carries real boundaries: what they bring in, what they execute, and what they stand behind.
about.measureStep2: Who advanced it into structured work
about.measureStep4: What evidence exists and who reviewed it
about.mastheadKicker: On the network we are building
about.formalLede: The full system as a mathematical tuple — every component named, every relation explicit, every state auditable.
about.formalCaption: The full eleven-tuple with relation constraints is defined in docs/architecture/11-formal-definition.md. In short: identity (I) admits nodes (N) into roles (R); roles execute deals (D) and tasks (T) under policy (G); proof (P) gates settlement (S); learning (L) feeds the policy back; agents (A) and external systems (X) are first-class members.
about.timelineDesc: Every prerequisite to coordinate global capital, projects, and services with verified outcomes is suddenly available — at the same time, for the first time.
about.year2018Body: USDC mainnet launches; institutional stablecoin rails go live.
about.year2020Body: Wyoming passes the DAO LLC Act; on-chain coordination gets legal infrastructure.
about.year2022Body: Sign-In With Ethereum (EIP-4361) finalizes; verifiable identity at protocol level.
about.year2024Body: GPT-4 class agents become cheap enough to run at task granularity. Verification is the bottleneck, not labor.
about.year2026Body: WCN integrates the prior decade into one protocol with one audit trail. The pieces existed; the connective tissue did not.
about.archDesc: Every interaction with WCN — from a wallet sign-in to a settlement entry — flows through the same audited layer stack. No back doors. No side channels.
about.archLayer3Sub: Three-ledger model, policy engine, learning loop.
about.archLayer4Sub: Every state transition is a typed event. Replayable. Auditable.
about.archLayer5Sub: Postgres of record. Outbox to event store. Cold archive.
about.genesisQuote: We are not building a marketplace. We are building the connective tissue between proof and capital — so the people who do the work capture the value.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.metaDesc: How WCN works: opportunities enter through nodes, work is structured and executed by people and AI agents, outcomes are verified with evidence, and settlement rewards real contribution.
howItWorks.headline: From opportunity to proof — in one loop.
howItWorks.lede: WCN is a business loop: structure work, execute with people and AI agents, verify with evidence, and record Proof of Business — so settlement always follows contribution.
howItWorks.subLede: Every step below is live in the product: nodes handle intake, tasks and deals carry execution, the Proof Desk handles evidence, and PoB connects operations to long-term value.
howItWorks.operatingLoopDesc: Every step is visible, reviewable, and traceable. Skip one and the chain breaks — that is the point.
howItWorks.archTitle: Five layers, from people to assets
howItWorks.archDesc: The stack is widest where people and agents touch reality, and tightest where assets and settlement must stay tied to proof.
howItWorks.bridgeEntryBody: Without an accountable operator, intake is just noise. Nodes declare what they bring so every task and outcome can be traced.
howItWorks.bridgeProofBody: Outcomes become records only after evidence and review. That is what keeps settlement tied to reality.
howItWorks.ctaDesc: Apply as a node to enter the network, or read the wiki for a deeper walkthrough.
howItWorks.loopStep1Body: Capital, projects, services, and relationships enter through accountable node operators.
howItWorks.loopStep2Body: Opportunities become tasks with owners, milestones, and clear definitions of done.
howItWorks.loopStep3Body: People carry judgment and trust; AI agents scale research, drafting, and operations.
howItWorks.loopStep4Body: Contracts, logs, and on-chain references are reviewed before any claim is accepted.
howItWorks.loopStep5Body: Verified outcomes become Proof of Business — an auditable record, not a score.
howItWorks.loopStep6Body: Verified PoB drives allocation. Governance and staking extend the same contribution logic.
howItWorks.layer1Detail: Where capital, deals, and services enter with a named responsible operator.
howItWorks.layer2Detail: AI automation that stays attributable and auditable.
howItWorks.layer3Detail: The Proof Desk decides what the system is allowed to record.
howItWorks.layer4Detail: Distribution is tied to verified contribution curves.
howItWorks.layer5Detail: Long-term instruments and liquidity built on verified history.

nodes.title: Node Network — WCN
nodes.metaDesc: Nodes are the operators who power WCN: they bring capital, projects, and services, own execution risk, and earn settlement based on verified outcomes.
nodes.headline: The operators who power the network.
nodes.lede: In WCN, a node is the accountable operator behind every deal, every task, and every outcome. Tiers reflect scope and trust — earned through verified results, not vanity metrics.
nodes.subLede: Nodes are where the business loop lands in the real economy: they bring in opportunities, own execution, provide evidence, and participate in settlement.
nodes.clarityTitle: What nodes are — and are not
nodes.hierarchyDesc: Aligned with on-chain registry enums (GLOBAL → AGENT): scope and trust from network anchors down to scoped AI execution.
nodes.lifecycleDesc: Five stages from first contact to full operations — with clear criteria at every gate.
nodes.ctaTitle: Ready to operate as a node?
nodes.ctaDesc: Submit an application, or read the wiki for deeper context on architecture and settlement.
nodes.resp1Body: Capital, projects, services, and relationships enter through the node's declared scope.
nodes.resp2Body: Nodes own the work: tasks, collaborators, milestones, and delivery risk.
nodes.resp3Body: Outcomes are backed by evidence. Verified loops become Proof of Business records.
nodes.resp4Body: Nodes participate in allocation and fees tied to their verified contribution.
nodes.tier1Desc: Cross-region capital, flagship projects, and strategic connectors. Example: sovereign funds, top-tier VCs, global infrastructure providers.
nodes.tier2Desc: National or financial-center hubs for compliance, capital formation, and deal orchestration. Example: Singapore fund admins, Dubai family offices.
nodes.tier3Desc: Geography-anchored operators: local deal flow, partnerships, and execution in a specific metro or market. Example: a capital desk in Shanghai, an OTC desk in Dubai.
nodes.tier4Desc: Vertical specialists who carry a sector thesis across regions. Example: DeFi research labs, RWA platforms, media networks, stablecoin programs.
nodes.tier5Desc: Service providers who plug into the network. Example: market makers, law firms, audit practices, development studios.
nodes.tier6Desc: Registered AI agents executing scoped tasks with permission, logging, and audit. Human oversight stays in the loop.
nodes.life1Body: Submit your identity, capabilities, jurisdiction, and the resources you bring.
nodes.life2Body: The network verifies your background, screens for risk, and may request clarifications.
nodes.life3Body: Sign agreements, set up billing, and meet go-live criteria before operations begin.
nodes.life4Body: Run tasks, close deals, submit evidence, and build your PoB record.
nodes.life5Body: Move up through verified contribution quality — or face probation for sustained failure.
nodes.govAdmissionText: Identity, capability, resource scope, and optional stake or seat binding when required.
nodes.govUpgradeText: Earned through PoB volume, evidence quality, and collaboration success — not marketing claims.
nodes.govRemovalText: Triggered by low contribution, fabricated evidence, severe breach, or settlement bypass.
nodes.areBullet1: Accountable operators who bring capital, projects, and services into the network
nodes.areBullet2: Owners of execution risk within their declared scope
nodes.areBullet3: Participants in evidence, review, and Proof of Business
nodes.areBullet4: Contributors to settlement, tied to what they actually delivered
nodes.notBullet1: Passive listings or unverified partner badges
nodes.notBullet2: Anonymous channels with no identity or accountability
nodes.notBullet3: Campaign shells with no ongoing role in the network
nodes.notBullet4: A substitute for legal, tax, or regulatory obligations

pob.title: Proof of Business (PoB)
pob.metaDesc: Proof of Business is WCN's verification layer: real outcomes are evidenced, reviewed, attributed, and recorded — creating an auditable path to fair settlement.
pob.headline: Only verified outcomes become value.
pob.lede: PoB is WCN's verification layer. It recognizes business outcomes that can be proved, attributed, and audited — so settlement always reflects what actually happened.
pob.subLede: PoB turns real, verifiable results into records the system can admit, track, and use as input for settlement. If it cannot be evidenced, it does not count.
pob.boundariesDesc: PoB rewards completed outcomes. If it cannot be evidenced and reviewed, it does not enter the record.
pob.verificationDesc: Five steps take an outcome from raw claim to system-confirmed record: submission, evidence, review, conclusion, and archive.
pob.proofDeskDesc: Proof Desk is where evidence is collected, reviews are queued, disputes are resolved, and final decisions are issued before anything becomes a PoB record.
pob.intakeDesc: Evidence packages tied to specific deals and tasks
pob.reviewDesc: Authenticity checks, clarifications, and policy compliance
pob.disputesDesc: A clear path to contest outcomes or attribution
pob.conclusionsDesc: Approve, request more info, or reject — every decision is explicit
pob.scoringDesc: In plain terms: PoB score = how big was the outcome, how important was it, how strong is the evidence, how well did it hold up, minus any risk flags. The formula below makes this precise.
pob.scoringFoot: Phase 1 focuses on evidence quality and ledger integrity. Token settlement and governance extensions arrive in Phase 2.
pob.attributionBody: Without attribution, PoB only proves a result exists. With it, the network sees who advanced the outcome — essential for fair credit and future allocation.
pob.notLawBody: PoB is an operational layer inside WCN. It does not replace securities, tax, or licensing obligations — teams still need proper counsel.
pob.ctaDesc: Apply as a node to start creating PoB records, or read the wiki for definitions and edge cases.
pob.pillar1Body: Only verified outcomes enter the PoB record. Activity without a provable result stays outside.
pob.pillar2Body: PoB tracks who led, who collaborated, and what made the result hold — not a flat participation score.
pob.pillar3Body: Recorded PoB becomes the structured input for allocation, fees, and governance.
pob.pillar4Body: Validated outcomes persist as durable records — not disappearing chat logs or campaign dashboards.
pob.rewarded6: Agent execution (logged work with attributable results)
pob.notRewarded1: Posts, hype, and activity without outcomes
pob.notRewarded3: Bot traffic, wash trading, and circular attribution
pob.notRewarded4: Side deals that bypass system attribution
pob.verificationStep1Body: Register the completed deal or task with clear context and a definition of done.
pob.verificationStep2Body: Attach structured proof: contracts, on-chain references, dashboards, or acceptance records.
pob.verificationStep3Body: Reviewers check authenticity, completeness, timeline, and attribution.
pob.verificationStep4Body: Approve, request more evidence, reject, or route to disputes.
pob.verificationStep5Body: Accepted records enter the PoB ledger as durable inputs for reputation and settlement.
pob.factorBaseValueHint: How large is the verified outcome.
pob.factorBusinessWeightHint: How strategically important this type of outcome is.
pob.factorQualityMultHint: Evidence depth, confirmation count, and audit readiness.
pob.factorTimeMultHint: How well the outcome holds up over time.
pob.factorRiskDiscountHint: Disputes, anomalies, and flags reduce the score.

auth.signInToAccount: Sign in to your WCN account
auth.oauthAccountLinked: An account with this email already exists. Please sign in with your original method.
auth.applyDesc: Join the WCN network as a node operator.
auth.signupSubtext: Create your account to get started

apply.metaDescription: Join the WCN network as a node operator. Apply as a capital, project, service, regional, media, or industry node.
apply.headline: Apply as a node.
apply.heroDesc: WCN is an application-based network. Tell us about your organization and resources — our team reviews every application within 48 hours.
apply.step1Desc: Tell us about your organization, resources, and how you plan to participate.
apply.step2Desc: We evaluate fit, capabilities, and alignment with the network.
apply.step3Desc: Qualified applicants join a 30-minute call to discuss scope and expectations.
apply.step4Desc: Seat assignment, permissions, and full platform access.
apply.capitalDesc: VCs, family offices, funds, and angel investors
apply.projectDesc: Protocols, DApps, infrastructure, and AI projects
apply.serviceDesc: Legal, audit, security, development, and growth firms
apply.regionalDesc: Country leads, city hubs, and local network connectors
apply.mediaDesc: Media outlets, KOLs, communities, and event organizers
apply.industryDesc: Exchanges, market makers, custodians, and data providers
apply.tipMessage: Applications that describe specific resources and explain how they align with the WCN network have a significantly higher approval rate.
apply.ctaDesc: Submit your application above. Our review team verifies every node before admission to the registry.
apply.successBody: Your application is in the queue. Our review team verifies every node before admission to the registry — expect a reply within 48 hours.
apply.fieldTerritoryPh: e.g. Singapore, Hong Kong, UAE, Southeast Asia
apply.fieldPastCasesPh: Describe 2–3 relevant deals, projects, or partnerships you have been involved in.
apply.fieldReferencesPh: Names and contacts of 1–2 references who can vouch for your work.
apply.fieldBoundaryHint: What you will and will not do as a node. Helps set expectations.
apply.fieldBoundaryPh: e.g. I will focus on sourcing seed-stage DeFi projects in SEA. I will not handle legal or compliance work.
apply.fieldWhyPh: What drew you to WCN? How do you see yourself contributing to and benefiting from the network?

reviewQueue.empty: No nodes are currently in these review stages.
reviewQueue.emptyFiltered: No nodes match the current stage filter or search.
reviewQueue.truncatedNotice: Only the {limit} most recently updated nodes are loaded here. The queue has {total} nodes total — narrow with search or open the node registry for full lists.
reviewQueue.casesIntro: Typical situations in this queue — use them to align checks and next steps.
reviewQueue.casesBullet1: Submitted: profile ready for triage; verify materials and routing before moving to under review.
reviewQueue.casesBullet2: Under review: diligence in progress; record decisions and follow-ups in the review workspace.
reviewQueue.casesBullet3: Need more info: request specifics from the applicant; keep the node here until requirements are met.
reviewQueue.casesHowItWorks: How the network operating loop works

wiki.description: WCN complete knowledge base — from project introduction to system architecture, fully understand the Web3 Capital Network.
wiki.heading: WCN Wiki
wiki.subtitle: From project definition to system architecture, from node mechanisms to business models — fully understand the design and operation of the Web3 Capital Network.

whyNow.title: Why Now — WCN
whyNow.metaDesc: WCN does not create demand. It responds to four structural ruptures and five windows that the industry already faces.
whyNow.headline: We did not invent the need. We answer ruptures that already exist.
whyNow.lede: WCN does not manufacture demand. It responds to systemic ruptures the industry already lives with, at the one moment the conditions to repair them have matured together.
whyNow.rupturesDesc: These are not opinions. They are the load-bearing failures of how Web3 organizes resources today.
whyNow.rupture1Body: Projects, capital, service providers, media, and liquidity sit scattered. None of them share one accountability chain or one chain of coordination.
whyNow.rupture2Body: Most opportunities stall at the introduction. They never enter sustained execution, and they never reach a closed loop.
whyNow.rupture3Body: The people who actually drive an outcome cannot be recorded, attributed, or settled by any system. Their work disappears.
whyNow.rupture4Body: Agents can already do the work. They lack a permission structure, accountability boundaries, logging requirements, and the right to settle.
whyNow.rupturesSynthesis: Web3 does not lack projects, capital, or content. What it lacks is a system that places all of them into one chain of collaboration, verification, and settlement.
whyNow.windowsDesc: WCN is worth building now not because the idea is new, but because the conditions matured at the same time.
whyNow.window1Body: Raw technology no longer guarantees success. Survival now depends on securing capital, forming markets, and organizing resources end to end.
whyNow.window2Title: Agents can execute, but cannot settle
whyNow.window2Body: Frontier models and agent frameworks already handle research, diligence, outreach, and growth. They still lack a commercial layer to settle results.
whyNow.window3Body: After airdrop and vanity-metric cycles, partners and capital are hard to persuade with concept alone. They now weigh real business and real revenue.
whyNow.window4Body: RWA, stablecoin rails, institutional entry, and settlement upgrades make the question of how assets are organized and served suddenly urgent.
whyNow.window5Body: Better workflow tools, mature node coordination, structured deal flow, and usable AI execution make building the network first finally feasible.
whyNow.ctaTitle: Build on the conditions, not the hype
whyNow.ctaDesc: If the diagnosis matches what you see, the response is already specified. Apply to operate a node, or read the full whitepaper.

economics.title: Economics — Value Capture & Settlement | WCN
economics.metaDesc: How WCN captures value: node-equity sales, Carry settlement, and service pools. The raise is derived in-document — a USD 55–60M median, 5–7 year runway, no token dependency.
economics.headline: Value is not captured by promises — it is settled by the ledger.
economics.lede: First prove the model survives without a token. Only then discuss one. The numbers below are derived in the document, not asserted.
economics.sourcesDesc: Value enters the system through three settled channels, not through a single speculative instrument.
economics.source1Body: The six-tier pyramid sells seats at one-time, tiered prices. These sales form the Foundation's base funding — the floor that does not depend on any future issuance.
economics.source2Body: Net profit per verified loop is distributed by the §08 waterfall: Backers 70%, Sourcing & Coordination 10%, Lead Node Bonus 10%, WCN Treasury 5%, Service Provider Pool 5%.
economics.source3Body: The Service Provider Pool and the Sourcing & Coordination bucket convert execution contribution into settled shares — work that is recorded, attributed, and paid.
economics.raiseDesc: The theoretical range is the envelope of seat counts and prices. The red-team median below is reproducible: per tier, a representative price times a conservative fill rate.
economics.tableCaption: Red-team derivation — seats × representative price × conservative fill rate = expected.
economics.raiseFootnote: Full-price total $104.4M sits inside the $82–127M envelope. Expected median $55.9M sits inside the $55–60M band. Fill rates are conservative red-team assumptions.
economics.tokenDesc: Funding sustainability does not depend on a token. A token is deferred to Phase 3 (M24+), compliance-gated, and fully allocated with no hidden share.
economics.tokenAllocCaption: Suggested allocation — 100% accounted for, no hidden share.
economics.alloc1Note: Founding / Country / City / Track governance-coordination right.
economics.tokenDisclaimerTitle: What the node reserve is not
economics.tokenDisclaimer: The node reserve is a governance-coordination right. It is not a fundraising tool and not a promise of appreciation. It activates only if Phase-3 compliance is met; otherwise it is void.
economics.tokenLegalNote: Securities and financial-instrument classification is determined by external counsel, per jurisdiction, at issuance. Percentages are suggested; final parameters follow the governance and legal process.
economics.moatDesc: Being first to combine the parts is an opportunity, not a wall — combinations can be copied. The real moat is a cumulative asset that thickens with time.
economics.moat1Body: Each verified loop sharpens how the network prices deal quality and node reputation. Later entrants do not hold this data.
economics.moat2Body: One partner per country holds exclusive coordination rights. Once a country is taken, it is taken.
economics.moat3Body: The multi-jurisdiction compliance structure accumulated by Phase 3 is hard for a follower to reproduce quickly.
economics.moatClose: The node network and the PoB ledger compound together — network-effect data, reputation, and agent-training that build on a first-mover lead.
economics.ctaTitle: Read the model. Then judge it.
economics.ctaDesc: The economics are open to inspection. Apply as a node, or read the full derivation and waterfall in the wiki.

governance.title: Governance
governance.metaDesc: Founder-led, but constrained by a constitution. An eight-layer command stack, two thresholds on the founder, and six things no majority can vote away.
governance.headline: Founder-led, but constrained by a constitution — not a personality cult.
governance.lede: Strong leadership, integrity checks, competence accountability. The constraints are written down, and the founder is inside them.
governance.stackDesc: Eight layers, L0 to L7. The constitution sits above the founder. Each layer below carries a defined mandate, not discretion.
governance.layer0Body: The highest constraint. It defines the non-votable items and the bounds on every waterfall parameter.
governance.layer1Body: Fiduciary duty and oversight. The board guards the constitution, not the roadmap.
governance.layer2Body: A 100-seat governance body. It holds the votes that constrain the founder.
governance.layer3Body: Commander. Strategy and final coordination, inside the bounds the layers above set.
governance.layer4Body: Daily execution and external delivery against the agreed strategy.
governance.layer5Body: Structured task execution. Every action retains a Proof-of-Behavior record.
governance.layer6Body: Regional and track coordination, connecting the network to local ground.
governance.layer7Body: Professional service and basic participation. The broad base of the stack.
governance.thresholdsDesc: We do not hide that this is founder-led. The checks split in two: integrity can trigger removal; competence can trigger mandatory review.
governance.integrityTitle: Integrity — removable, at a very high bar
governance.integrityDesc: The founder can be removed, but only through three gates clearing at once. The bar is deliberately near-absolute.
governance.integrityTriggers: Triggers are limited to criminal conviction, medical incapacity, or financial fraud.
governance.competenceDesc: Sustained underperformance does not remove the founder. It forces a strategic review in the open, with a remediation plan.
governance.competenceTriggers: This closes the gap left when only criminal conduct can act as a backstop.
governance.analogy: Think Vitalik to the Ethereum Foundation: removable in theory, irreplaceable in practice. The competence check ensures irreplaceable does not mean unaccountable.
governance.neverEyebrow: Six things no majority can vote away
governance.neverTitle: Below these, the vote does not reach
governance.neverDesc: Six items sit outside majority rule. No motion, however large the majority, can override them.
governance.never1Body: A node's core privacy rights cannot be violated by majority vote.
governance.never2Body: Anti-money-laundering and compliance enforcement cannot be bypassed.
governance.never3Body: A formed Proof-of-Behavior record cannot be altered after the fact.
governance.never4Body: A signed legal agreement cannot be unilaterally breached.
governance.never5Body: An emergency motion that harms absent minority nodes cannot pass.
governance.never6Body: A short-term payout that damages long-term sustainability cannot pass.
governance.ctaDesc: The stack, the thresholds, and the non-votable items are public. Apply to take a seat, or read the full whitepaper.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.metaDesc: Milestone-gated, not date-promised. Three phases, each exiting only when its criteria are met. The token arrives in Phase 3 — not before.
roadmap.headline: Milestone-gated, not date-promised.
roadmap.lede: Each phase exits only when its criteria are met. The token arrives in Phase 3 — not before. These are plans, not guarantees.
roadmap.phasesTitle: Network first, then rules, then onchain
roadmap.phasesDesc: Three phases run in sequence. Each carries a window and an exit gate. A phase advances only when its gate is cleared.
roadmap.phase1Focus: Stand up 100 Founding seats, open node registration, and close the first Proof-of-Business.
roadmap.phase2Focus: Roll out Country, City, and Track nodes, turning the network into a repeatable system.
roadmap.phase3Focus: Issue the token, clear cross-jurisdiction compliance, and begin the DAO transition.
roadmap.phase3Exit2: 5-jurisdiction compliance (target: Singapore / Hong Kong / UAE / Switzerland / EU)
roadmap.daysTitle: What happens before the network exists
roadmap.daysDesc: The opening quarter is concrete. Four checkpoints carry the work from founder decisions to a closed PoB loop.
roadmap.day14Body: The founder settles five key calls: GP role, Agent downgrade, US blocking, node candidates, and founder narrative.
roadmap.day30Body: Foundation registration starts, and the Track Record Pack is complete.
roadmap.day60Body: The first 10 nodes are signed, General Counsel hiring is underway, and Foundation Charter v0.1 is drafted.
roadmap.day90Body: The first PoB loop closes — a nameable deal from the founder's existing pipeline — and whitepaper v2.1 is published.
roadmap.closing: Network first, then rules, then onchain. These are plans, not guarantees, and the protocol never promises returns.
roadmap.ctaDesc: The phases, the gates, and the first 90 days are public. Apply to take a seat, or read the full whitepaper.
